channel.title: Space technology of the Day
channel.description: RSS feed of the space technology of the day.

item.title: Satellite delay
item.description: Satellite delay is the noticeable latency due to the limited speed of light, when sending data to and from satellites, especially distant geosynchronous satellites. Bouncing a signal off a geosynchronous satellite takes about a quarter of a second, which is enough to be noticeable, but relaying data between two or three such satellites increases the delay.
